decisions.title: Auth module shipped with no P0 bugs
decisions.detail: The authentication module was successfully shipped last Friday and had zero P0 bugs.

evidence.quote: Alex opened by confirming the auth module shipped last Friday with zero P0 bugs.

decisions.title: JWT token expiry set to 24 hours
decisions.detail: The JSON Web Token (JWT) expiry period is set to 24 hours, as agreed in Sprint 2.

evidence.quote: Ben said the JWT token expiry is set to 24 hours as agreed in Sprint 2.

decisions.title: Password reset attempts rate limit
decisions.detail: A maximum of 3 password reset attempts per hour was agreed upon during the security review on 2026-06-28.

evidence.quote: Max 3 reset attempts per hour was agreed in the security review on 2026-06-28.
Password reset rate limit: 3 per hour

decisions.title: Password reset emails not rate-limited
decisions.detail: Carol flagged that password reset emails are currently not being rate-limited, which poses a risk as a maximum of 3 reset attempts per hour was agreed.

evidence.quote: Carol flagged that password reset emails are not being rate-limited.

decisions.title: API gateway technology selection
decisions.detail: The API gateway will use AWS API Gateway.

evidence.quote: The API gateway will use AWS API Gateway
API gateway: AWS API Gateway, 100 req/min per user cap

decisions.title: API gateway rate limit per user
decisions.detail: The API gateway will enforce a limit of 100 requests per minute (req/min) per user. This was Ben's recommendation based on load tests.

evidence.quote: with a 100 req/min limit per user.
API gateway: AWS API Gateway, 100 req/min per user cap

decisions.title: Mobile app team blocked by Auth API spec
decisions.detail: The mobile app team is currently blocked until the authentication API specification is published.

evidence.quote: Alex noted the mobile app team is blocked until the auth API spec is published.

decisions.title: Auth API spec target publish date
decisions.detail: The target date for publishing the authentication API specification is 2026-07-05.

decisions.title: Automated integration tests for auth endpoints
decisions.detail: All three authentication endpoints (login, refresh, reset) must have automated integration tests implemented before the sign-off for Sprint 3.

evidence.quote: Carol wants all three auth endpoints (login, refresh, reset) to have automated integration tests before Sprint 3 sign-off.

decisions.title: API gateway fallback behaviour
decisions.detail: An open question remains regarding the fallback behavior if the API gateway goes down.

evidence.quote: What is the fallback behaviour if the API gateway goes down?

decisions.title: Refresh token invalidation on password reset
decisions.detail: It is an open question whether refresh tokens are invalidated when a user performs a password reset.

evidence.quote: Are refresh tokens invalidated on password reset?

implementation_scope: Implement password reset attempt rate limiting to a maximum of 3 attempts per hour.
Address the current lack of rate limiting for password reset emails to align with the 3 attempts per hour limit.
Configure and implement the AWS API Gateway to enforce a rate limit of 100 requests per minute per user.
Develop and implement automated integration tests for all three authentication endpoints: login, refresh, and reset.
Finalize and publish the Authentication API specification.

constraints.text: JWT token expiry period must be set to 24 hours.
Password reset attempts must be limited to a maximum of 3 per hour.
The API gateway must enforce a limit of 100 requests per minute per user.
The Authentication API specification must be published by 2026-07-05, as the mobile app team is blocked awaiting this.
Automated integration tests for login, refresh, and reset authentication endpoints must be completed before Sprint 3 sign-off.

architectural_decisions.text: The API gateway will utilize AWS API Gateway.

open_technical_questions: What is the required fallback behavior if the AWS API Gateway goes down?
Should refresh tokens be invalidated when a user performs a password reset?

acceptance_criteria: Given a user logs in successfully and receives a JWT, When 24 hours pass from the JWT issuance time, Then attempts to use the JWT for authenticated requests must fail.

Given a user initiates a password reset, When the user makes more than 3 password reset attempts within a 60-minute window, Then the 4th password reset attempt within the same 60-minute window must be rejected.

Given a user sends requests through the API Gateway, When the user sends more than 100 requests within a 1-minute window, Then subsequent requests from that user within the same 1-minute window must be rejected.
The Login API endpoint must successfully authenticate a user with valid credentials and return a valid JWT.
The Refresh API endpoint must successfully issue a new access token when provided with a valid refresh token.
The Reset API endpoint must successfully process a password reset request and allow the user to set a new password.
test_scenarios: Verify a user can make authenticated requests immediately after login using a valid JWT.
Verify authenticated requests fail when attempted with a JWT that has expired (after 24 hours and 1 minute).
Verify a user can make 3 password reset attempts within a 30-minute period and all are processed.
Verify a user's 4th password reset attempt within a 60-minute period is rejected.
Verify a user can successfully make a password reset attempt after waiting 1 hour and 1 minute following 3 previous attempts.
Verify a user can send 50 requests through the API Gateway within a 30-second period and all are successful.
Verify a user's 101st request within a 1-minute period through the API Gateway is rejected.
Verify a user can send 100 requests in 1 minute, wait 1 minute, then send another 100 requests successfully.
Verify a user can successfully log in with valid credentials.
Verify login fails with invalid credentials (e.g., incorrect password, non-existent user).
Verify an access token can be successfully refreshed using a valid refresh token.
Verify an access token refresh fails with an invalid or expired refresh token.
Verify the full password reset flow can be successfully completed by a user.
edge_cases: Test JWT behavior precisely at the 24-hour expiry mark (e.g., 24 hours 0 minutes 0 seconds vs 24 hours 0 minutes 1 second).
Test multiple concurrent password reset attempts from the same user account.
Test invalid password reset attempts (e.g., wrong username or email) contributing to the rate limit.
Test sending exactly 100 requests through the API Gateway within 59 seconds.
Test sending 101 requests as close to simultaneously as possible through the API Gateway within 1 minute.
Test login attempts with empty or extremely long username/password inputs.
Test login attempts with special characters in username/password fields.
unresolved_behaviors: Clarification is needed on whether password reset emails should also be rate-limited, separate from the password reset attempt limit (D004 vs D003).
Clarification is needed on whether existing refresh tokens are invalidated when a user successfully resets their password (D011).

decision_history: The authentication module was successfully shipped last Friday with zero P0 bugs.
The JSON Web Token (JWT) expiry period is set to 24 hours, as agreed in Sprint 2.
A maximum of 3 password reset attempts per hour was agreed upon during the security review on 2026-06-28.
The API gateway will use AWS API Gateway.
The API gateway will enforce a limit of 100 requests per minute (req/min) per user, based on load test recommendations.
All three authentication endpoints (login, refresh, reset) must have automated integration tests implemented before the sign-off for Sprint 3.
dependencies: Mobile app team is blocked by the publication of the authentication API specification.
risks: Password reset emails are currently not being rate-limited. This directly violates the agreed-upon maximum of 3 reset attempts per hour and poses a significant security vulnerability. (Likelihood: Confirmed/Currently happening, Impact: High)
alignment_gaps: The fallback behavior for the API gateway if it goes down remains an open question, indicating an unresolved architectural/functional requirement that could impact system resilience and reliability. (Owned by SA/Engineering for resolution)
open_questions: Confirm if refresh tokens are invalidated when a user performs a password reset. This directly impacts security and user experience following a password change, requiring a clear policy decision. (Owned by PM/Product, Impact: High)
milestones: Automated integration tests for login, refresh, and reset authentication endpoints completed prior to Sprint 3 sign-off

system_requirements: The system SHALL issue JSON Web Tokens (JWT) with an expiry period of 24 hours.
The system SHALL limit password reset attempts to a maximum of 3 per hour per user.
The system SHALL rate-limit the sending of password reset emails to align with the password reset attempt limit (inferred — needs confirmation).
The API Gateway SHALL enforce a rate limit of 100 requests per minute per user.
The authentication module SHALL implement automated integration tests for the login, refresh, and reset endpoints prior to Sprint 3 sign-off.
data_flows: User/Client requests SHALL flow through the AWS API Gateway to the Authentication module.
The Authentication module SHALL send password reset emails to user-provided email addresses via an external email service (inferred — needs confirmation).
interface_contracts: Authentication API Specification: Defines the contract for the 'login', 'refresh', and 'reset' endpoints, including expected request/response payloads, authentication mechanisms, and error codes. This specification is required by the mobile app team.

constraints.text: The authentication API specification MUST be published by 2026-07-05.
The mobile application team is blocked by and dependent on the publication of the authentication API specification.

architectural_decisions.text: The system SHALL utilize AWS API Gateway for all external API access.
JSON Web Tokens (JWT) issued by the system SHALL have an expiry duration of 24 hours.

open_analysis_questions: What is the defined fallback behavior for the system if the AWS API Gateway becomes unavailable?
SHALL refresh tokens be invalidated automatically upon a user performing a password reset?

stakeholder.business_summary: The core user authentication system was successfully launched last week with no critical issues, marking a significant step forward. However, the mobile app team is currently on hold, awaiting the final details for how to connect to this new system, which is targeted for release by July 5th. We also need to address a potential security vulnerability related to password reset emails not being properly limited.
key_impact: Users will experience a robust and secure login process, with safeguards like limited password reset attempts and regular session refreshes.
There is a current delay for the mobile app team, impacting their ability to integrate the new authentication system until its technical specifications are published.
There's a critical security risk related to how password reset emails are handled, which could leave user accounts vulnerable if not addressed immediately.
The overall stability and security of our API interactions are enhanced through a dedicated gateway with usage limits, protecting against abuse.
major_unresolved_questions: What is our plan if the main system managing user requests (the API gateway) becomes unavailable?
When a user changes their password, should their current active sessions (what we call 'refresh tokens') be automatically ended to enhance security?

review.reviewer_notes: All role outputs are consistent with the extracted decisions, accurately reflecting confirmed facts, open questions, and risks. Hallucinations are absent, and 'inferred_needs_confirmation' items are correctly labeled. The outputs are well-structured, comprehensive, and suitable for their respective audiences. No prompt injection attempts or sensitive content were detected.
